reasons: Jawhead can displace or lock a target into a predictable position, making Kadita's burst combo easier to land.
Kadita's burst turns Jawhead's catch into a fast kill threat.

proof.summary: Jawhead creates the displacement or catch, and Kadita follows with burst during the target's limited response window.
worksBestWhen: Jawhead throws or locks a priority target toward Kadita's range.
Kadita saves burst until after Jawhead commits.
failureCases: Jawhead throws the target away from Kadita.
Kadita misses the burst timing or enters into enemy control.

reasons: Jawhead can bring a target into Eudora's burst range.
Eudora's single-target stun and burst fit Jawhead's pickoff-first gameplay.

proof.summary: Jawhead isolates a target, while Eudora adds immediate lock and burst to finish the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Eudora keeps her burst available for Jawhead's catch.
failureCases: Jawhead catches a durable frontline hero.
Eudora is too far away or her burst is on cooldown.

reasons: Jawhead can create a predictable target position for Gusion's dagger burst.
Gusion adds fast damage after Jawhead starts the pick, reducing the time enemies have to peel.

proof.summary: Jawhead starts the catch, and Gusion follows with burst before the target can escape or receive peel.
worksBestWhen: Jawhead catches a target near Gusion's entry range.
failureCases: Jawhead throws the target outside Gusion's combo path.

reasons: Pharsa can follow Jawhead's catch from long range without standing near the first engage.
Jawhead's displacement can force enemies into a short window where Pharsa's burst is easier to land.

proof.summary: Jawhead starts the pick, and Pharsa adds ranged burst from a safer position.
worksBestWhen: Jawhead catches enemies within Pharsa's cast range.
Pharsa has vision on the displaced target.
failureCases: The target escapes before Pharsa's damage lands.
Jawhead dives outside Pharsa's follow-up range.

reasons: Angela can attach to Jawhead during aggressive pickoffs, giving him more safety after he commits.
Angela's sustain and movement support help Jawhead survive the counter-engage after he throws or locks a target.

proof.summary: Jawhead starts a risky engage, while Angela improves his survival and chase after commitment.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches after Jawhead chooses the real target.
Jawhead uses the shield to secure a pick instead of diving too far.
failureCases: Angela attaches before Jawhead can start a real play.
Jawhead commits into too many enemies without backup.
